stories.headline: DeFi coalition unveils 116,500 rsETH recovery blueprint for $292M Kelp DAO exploit
stories.tldr: A DeFi United-led group published a step-by-step plan to re-back the 116,500 unbacked rsETH minted in this month's Kelp DAO bridge hack.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: a credible coordinated fix could halt the contagion that has already cost Aave $6B+ in TVL and dragged DeFi's stack down $13B in 48 hours.
the_details: Plan targets restoration of backing for 116,500 unbacked rsETH minted via the forged cross-chain message.
Coalition includes Aave-aligned contributors after Aave V3 ate stolen-rsETH collateral on April 18-19.
Aave token still down 16% on the month; Kelp tab now $292M, the largest single exploit of 2026.

stories.headline: US judge denies Sam Bankman-Fried retrial bid, calls misconduct claims baseless
stories.tldr: A federal judge rejected Sam Bankman-Fried's motion for a new trial, dismissing his alleged new evidence and prosecutorial-misconduct narrative.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: closes the last clean appellate runway for SBF and locks in the legal precedent shaping every future US crypto-fraud case.
the_details: Judge ruled the proffered new evidence and misconduct allegations failed the threshold for retrial.
SBF remains on a 25-year sentence after his 2023 conviction on seven counts including wire fraud.
Ruling lands as the SEC-CFTC March 17 joint framework reshapes how fraud cases get charged.

stories.headline: Lightspark CEO David Marcus debuts Bitcoin wallet built for AI agents
stories.tldr: Lightspark unveiled a Bitcoin wallet that lets autonomous AI agents send and receive payments, built on Lightning rails.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: opens a programmable money lane for agent-to-agent commerce — the first credible BTC primitive aimed at the AI x payments stack.
the_details: Wallet announced at the 2026 Lightspark conference by CEO and ex-PayPal/Meta exec David Marcus.
Targets machine-speed micropayments via Lightning Network with agent-controlled keys.
Slots into the broader push to bridge AI inference billing onto crypto rails.

stories.headline: Tropykus sunsets V1 Bitcoin lending app, gives users until July to withdraw
stories.tldr: Bitcoin lending protocol Tropykus suspended deposits and new loans on its V1, urging users to pull funds before the July 2026 cutoff.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: another BTC-native lender retiring V1 underscores how thin the post-Celsius/BlockFi BTC-yield market has become.
the_details: Tropykus halted deposits and new originations on V1 effective immediately.
Hard withdrawal deadline set for July 2026; users left after that face manual recovery.
Fits a wider pattern of BTC-yield desks consolidating around Lightning and on-chain wrappers.

stories.headline: Wall Street arrives in force at Consensus Miami: institutions now 35% of audience, $10T AUM
stories.tldr: Morgan Stanley and JPMorgan are sponsoring Consensus Miami 2026, with institutional attendance nearly doubling to ~35% of the 15,000-strong crowd.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: TradFi flow desks aren't tourists anymore — sponsorship dollars from MS and JPM signal the institutional capture of crypto's flagship event.
the_details: Consensus Miami runs May 5-7, 2026 with 15,000+ expected attendees.
Institutional share of audience near 35%, representing roughly $10 trillion in AUM.
Roster includes federal policymakers and TradFi sponsors alongside crypto natives.
